On Thu, Mar 26, 2026 at 1:58 PM benjamin barber <starworks5@gmail.com>
wrote:

> Sure, you can go ahead and try this informal hearing, but it seems to me
> like just another excuse to delay, and it just sounds like the matter is
> going to have to go before a judge.
>
>
> With regards to the reasonable accommodation about the April 1st deadline
> to vacate:
>
> In November there was a contested restraining order hearing, and the
> restraining order was continued against julio
>
> In December I had placed an application to the blossom, which they sat on
> and did not send to you, which amounts to "lemon dropping" (the reverse of
> cherry picking), and what I suspect to be "lemon dropping" based on
> protected immutable characteristics given that is what employees claim
> happen on glassdoor, and what your office promotes as an official policy,
> in violation of 42 usc 1981
> https://dochub.clackamas.us/documents/drupal/ed77538a-1d00-45f2-b95b-9aecca79e5f3
>
> In January early I had received the 90 days notice and told your office
> that I wanted the tenant protection voucher, after incorrectly stating I
> had not put an application into blossom, which your office had also
> confirmed.
>
> In January your office violated the court order by removing me from the
> lease and putting Julio Cortez back on the lease
>
> In March you tried to meet your obligations to retain an apartment for us,
> by giving us a 3rd floor apartment with stairs by the same company that
> runs the blossom.
>
> Only last week did you finally provide me with a tenant protection
> voucher, so that I could actually move out, 12 days from the day from which
> I was supposed to be evicted, so in effect the conditions you want to
> complain about are circumstances of your own creation.
>
>
> Thus, my reasonable accommodation would be based on the illusory promise,
> promissory estoppel, frustration of purpose, force majure, and unclean
> hands.
>
> https://www.law.cornell.edu/wex/force_majeure
> https://www.law.cornell.edu/wex/frustration_of_purpose
> https://www.law.cornell.edu/wex/illusory_promise
> https://www.law.cornell.edu/wex/promissory_estoppel
> https://www.law.cornell.edu/wex/clean-hands_doctrine
>
>
> [image: image.png]

>> On Wed, Mar 25, 2026 at 7:41 PM Tilton, Kati <KTilton@clackamas.us>
>> wrote:
>>
>> Hello
>>
>> I wanted to follow up with a summary of the available housing options and
>> the required next steps. Please review carefully, as all options require a
>> move by *April 30*.
>>
>> *Option 1 – Porting to Multnomah County*
>>
>>    - HACC has submitted documentation to Multnomah County
>>    - *Next Step:* Home Forward will contact your Household.
>>    - Your Household will complete intake and orientation with Home
>>    Forward and request rental assistance through them
>>    - Your Household will search for and identify a unit
>>    - *Deadline: Must be moved to Multnomah County by 04/30*
>>
>> *Option 2 – Searching Independently in Clackamas County Using a Tenant
>> Protection Voucher (TPV) – see attached*
>>
>>    - *Next step:* Your Household searches for and identifies a unit
>>    - You may use your one-bedroom voucher to lease a two-bedroom unit so
>>    long as it is affordable for your household. Please refer to the Tenant
>>    Rent Subsidy Worksheet (TRSW) provided to you at orientation regarding
>>    affordability.
>>    - Potential option includes a market-rate unit, using the Tenant
>>    Protection Voucher (TPV) at Parkside Heights Building B, which is currently
>>    leasing, located on the same property as Blossom and Community Apartments.
>>
>>
>>    - https://www.liveparksideheights.com/
>>       parksideheightsmgr@quantumres.com
>>       <parksideheightsmgr@quantumes.com>
>>
>>
>>    - *Deadline: Must be moved to a new unit by 04/30*
>>
>> *Option 3 – Assigned PBV unit at Parkside Heights Building B*
>>
>>    - Based on the preliminary reasonable accommodation request
>>    (including a scheduled doctor’s appointment), the assigned unit will be
>>    moved from Easton Ridge to a PBV at Parkside Heights Building B.
>>    - Must remain in the unit for at least *24* months before requesting
>>    to go on a waitlist to receive a Tenant-based Voucher.
>>    - Relocation funds will be fully applied to this move
>>    - Unit type: One-bedroom
>>    - *Next step:* Your Household must notify HACC of your interest in a
>>    Project-Based Voucher, and HACC will refer you to the property.
>>    - *Deadline: Must be moved by 04/30*
>>
>> *If The Move Is Not Completed by 04/30*
>>
>>    - Eviction documentation will be filed with Clackamas County Court on
>>    04/01
>>
>>
>>    - Initial court hearing is expected in mid-April
>>
>>
>>    - A stipulated agreement can be reached to allow Your Household to
>>          vacate the public housing unit by 04/30
>>
>>
>>    - A sheriff’s lockout will be scheduled for early May
>>
>>
>>    - A lockout/FED (Forcible Entry and Detainer) will result in
>>          ineligibility for continued Rental Assistance
>>
>> As stated during your orientation, a decision regarding the need for a
>> larger voucher will not be made until appropriate reasonable accommodation
>> verification documentation has been received.
>>
>>
>>
>> Please let me know if you have any questions or need assistance with the
>> next steps.
>>
>>
>>
>> Thank you,
>>
>> Kati

>>
>>
>> Kati Tilton

On Thu, Mar 26, 2026 at 1:58 PM benjamin barber <starworks5@gmail.com> wrote:
Sure, you can go ahead and try this informal hearing, but it seems to me like just another excuse to delay, and it just sounds like the matter is going to have to go before a judge.


With regards to the reasonable accommodation about the April 1st deadline to vacate:

In November there was a contested restraining order hearing, and the restraining order was continued against julio

In December I had placed an application to the blossom, which they sat on and did not send to you, which amounts to "lemon dropping" (the reverse of cherry picking), and what I suspect to be "lemon dropping" based on protected immutable characteristics given that is what employees claim happen on glassdoor, and what your office promotes as an official policy, in violation of 42 usc 1981 https://dochub.clackamas.us/documents/drupal/ed77538a-1d00-45f2-b95b-9aecca79e5f3

In January early I had received the 90 days notice and told your office that I wanted the tenant protection voucher, after incorrectly stating I had not put an application into blossom, which your office had also confirmed.

In January your office violated the court order by removing me from the lease and putting Julio Cortez back on the lease

In March you tried to meet your obligations to retain an apartment for us, by giving us a 3rd floor apartment with stairs by the same company that runs the blossom. 

Only last week did you finally provide me with a tenant protection voucher, so that I could actually move out, 12 days from the day from which I was supposed to be evicted, so in effect the conditions you want to complain about are circumstances of your own creation. 


Thus, my reasonable accommodation would be based on the illusory promise, promissory estoppel, frustration of purpose, force majure, and unclean hands.

https://www.law.cornell.edu/wex/force_majeure
https://www.law.cornell.edu/wex/frustration_of_purpose
https://www.law.cornell.edu/wex/illusory_promise
https://www.law.cornell.edu/wex/promissory_estoppel
https://www.law.cornell.edu/wex/clean-hands_doctrine




On Thu, Mar 26, 2026 at 12:23 PM Tilton, Kati <KTilton@clackamas.us> wrote:

Hello,

 

If you disagree with the decision, you may request an informal hearing as outlined in the denial letter.

 

Please clarify what your reasonable accommodation request is in the following statement: “I also formally request a reasonable accommodation, given that the tenant protection voucher was only received last week, due to delays and violations of the violence against women act, by trying to remove me from the lease and keeping Julio on the lease, in violation of a court order, as the proximal cause of delays.”

Thank you,


Kati

 

Kati Tilton

971-201-8582

Operations Manager, Housing Authority of Clackamas County

 

From: benjamin barber <starworks5@gmail.com>
Sent: Thursday, March 26, 2026 10:37 AM
To: Tilton, Kati <KTilton@clackamas.us>; Williams, Billy <BWilliams@clackamas.us>
Subject: Re: HCV Orientation

 

Warning: External email. Be cautious opening attachments and links.

 

It is unsurprising that your office doesn't seem to be able to comply with laws, given that you spent several months of time violating the "violence against women act", and refusing to obey court orders, but I believe you should look at where your position has gotten other public housing agencies in the past, and your position is unreasonable. I also formally request a reasonable accommodation, given that the tenant protection voucher was only received last week, due to delays and violations of the violence against women act, by trying to remove me from the lease and keeping Julio on the lease, in violation of a court order, as the proximal cause of delays.

https://fhco.org/wp-content/uploads/2021/10/DisabilityGuide_FHCO_2022.pdf







https://www.hud.gov/sites/documents/14-25pihn.pdf






https://ecf.ksd.uscourts.gov/cgi-bin/show_public_doc?2022cv1180-15=

On Wed, Mar 25, 2026 at 7:41 PM Tilton, Kati <KTilton@clackamas.us> wrote:

Hello

I wanted to follow up with a summary of the available housing options and the required next steps. Please review carefully, as all options require a move by April 30.

Option 1 – Porting to Multnomah County

  • HACC has submitted documentation to Multnomah County
  • Next Step: Home Forward will contact your Household.
  • Your Household will complete intake and orientation with Home Forward and request rental assistance through them
  • Your Household will search for and identify a unit
  • Deadline: Must be moved to Multnomah County by 04/30

Option 2 – Searching Independently in Clackamas County Using a Tenant Protection Voucher (TPV) – see attached

  • Next step: Your Household searches for and identifies a unit
  • You may use your one-bedroom voucher to lease a two-bedroom unit so long as it is affordable for your household. Please refer to the Tenant Rent Subsidy Worksheet (TRSW) provided to you at orientation regarding affordability.
  • Potential option includes a market-rate unit, using the Tenant Protection Voucher (TPV) at Parkside Heights Building B, which is currently leasing, located on the same property as Blossom and Community Apartments.
    • https://www.liveparksideheights.com/
      parksideheightsmgr@quantumres.com
  • Deadline: Must be moved to a new unit by 04/30

Option 3 – Assigned PBV unit at Parkside Heights Building B

  • Based on the preliminary reasonable accommodation request (including a scheduled doctor’s appointment), the assigned unit will be moved from Easton Ridge to a PBV at Parkside Heights Building B.
  • Must remain in the unit for at least 24 months before requesting to go on a waitlist to receive a Tenant-based Voucher.
  • Relocation funds will be fully applied to this move
  • Unit type: One-bedroom
  • Next step: Your Household must notify HACC of your interest in a Project-Based Voucher, and HACC will refer you to the property.
  • Deadline: Must be moved by 04/30

If The Move Is Not Completed by 04/30

  • Eviction documentation will be filed with Clackamas County Court on 04/01
    • Initial court hearing is expected in mid-April
      • A stipulated agreement can be reached to allow Your Household to vacate the public housing unit by 04/30
    • A sheriff’s lockout will be scheduled for early May
      • A lockout/FED (Forcible Entry and Detainer) will result in ineligibility for continued Rental Assistance

As stated during your orientation, a decision regarding the need for a larger voucher will not be made until appropriate reasonable accommodation verification documentation has been received.

 

Please let me know if you have any questions or need assistance with the next steps.

 

Thank you,

Kati

 

Kati Tilton

971-201-8582

Operations Manager, Housing Authority of Clackamas County

 

From: benjamin barber <starworks5@gmail.com>
Sent: Wednesday, March 25, 2026 12:47 PM
To: Tilton, Kati <KTilton@clackamas.us>
Subject: Re: HCV Orientation

 

Warning: External email. Be cautious opening attachments and links.

 

My mom is still getting phone calls this morning about my brother, against whom a restraining order has been filed and granted by the court, and I dont know A) why you have people who are still calling her and not maintaining the policy of keeping communications in writing and B) making third party contact about her housing with a person against whom she has a restraining order issued in a court.

Moreover I have provided you with the reasonable accommodation paperwork, and I am still awaiting you to process that information. I have contacted waterleaf apartments in portland, and they have put us #4 on the waiting list for a two bedroom apartment, pending the ability to get the voucher issued by your department.

Thank you,

Kati
